mission.title: gitea: An internal CI/CD system compatible with Github actions workflow yaml syntax, action yaml syntax and most action plugins
mission.description: An internal CI/CD system compatible with Github actions workflow yaml syntax, action yaml syntax and most action plugins. Requested by 266+ users.

steps.title: Check current gitea setup
steps.description:
Verify your gitea version and configuration:
```bash
gitea --version
```
This feature requires a working gitea installation.

steps.title: Review gitea configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant gitea configuration:
# Internal CI/CD system

I propose to implement an internal CI/CD system that is compatible with Github workflows `yaml` syntax, action `yaml` syntax, and most action plugins.

steps.title: Apply the fix for An internal CI/CD system compatible with Github actions…
steps.description:
To diff from Github Actions, I don't think we should use actions as name. `Bots` might means `automatical` but not `robot`, I'm not English native speaker and maybe there is a better name for it. This is really not only a CI/CD system, you can do more like below yml file.
```yaml
name: Issue post

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  comment_created:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: env
      - name: Create comment
        uses:

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "An internal CI/CD system compatible with Github actions…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: To diff from Github Actions, I don't think we should use actions as name. `Bots` might means `automatical` but not `robot`, I'm not English native speaker and maybe there is a better name for it. This is really not only a CI/CD system, you can do more like below yml file.

prerequisites.description: A working gitea installation or development environment.
